title: trichopyrum intermedium subsp. epiroticum
intro: Trichopyrum intermedium subsp. epiroticum, also known as Epirotic or Intermediate Trichopyrum, is a subspecies of plant in the family Apiaceae, native to the Epirot region of Greece. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Trichopyrum intermedium subsp. epiroticum include relief from coughs, colds, and bronchitis, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate sinus congestion. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are prepared by steeping the leaves and roots in hot water or a solvent such as ethanol.
